commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh Behold, is it not of the LORD of hosts that the people shall labor to feed the fire, and the people shall weary themselves in vain. Things That is, shall not these punishments that are here recorded come from the Lord upon him that is guilty of such crimes. (Challoner) or, are not these riches from the Lord. The king of Tyre thought himself a god, Ezechiel xxviii. (Calmet) People; enemies of God's people. (Challoner) The riches of the Tyrians shall perish, so that the troops of Nabuchodonosor shall find nothing worth their trouble. Thus all were justly punished.

commentaries.author: Richard Challoner
commentaries.content:
Are not these things: That is, shall not these punishments that are here recorded, come from the Lord upon him that is guilty of such crimes.

The people shall labour: Viz., the enemies of God's people.
